elements.sent-2411-001: Harikumar { School of Physics, University of Hyderabad { Central University P.O, Hyderabad-500046, Telangana, India.}}
elements.sent-2411-002: In this paper, we construct Yang-Mills theory in the $\kappa$-space-time.
elements.sent-2411-003: We derive Yang-Mills equations in the $\kappa$-deformed space-time, valid up to first order in the deformation parameter $a$, using the generalisation of Feynman's approach.
elements.sent-2411-004: We first derive the $\kappa$-deformed Wong's equation and using it, in the Jacobi identity involving velocities and coordinates of $\kappa$-deformed space-time, we derive the $\kappa$-deformed homogeneous Yang-Mills equations.
elements.sent-2411-005: We then construct the Lagrangian for Yang-Mills theory in $\kappa$-deformed space-time and show that it is invariant under SU(N) transformation.
elements.sent-2411-006: We also derive the expression for the force experienced by an isospin-carrying particle in the presence of Yang-Mills field in the $\kappa$-space-time.
elements.sent-2411-007: Microscopic theory of gravity requires amalgamation of general relativity and quantum mechanics and such a theory is needed to understand and explain the nature of gravitational force at very short distances, well below nuclear size.
elements.sent-2411-008: Though many different approaches have been developed to discuss quantum gravity, with varying degrees of success, a completely satisfactory quantum theory of gravity still remains elusive.
elements.sent-2411-009: But one of the common features all these approaches share is the existence of a fundamental length scale; it is below this length scale, the quantum effects of gravity become important.
elements.sent-2411-010: As non-commutative space-time(s) inherently introduce a length scale, non-commutative geometry promises to be a viable framework for consistent quantisation of gravity \cite{connes .}
elements.sent-2411-011: Thus it is also interesting to construct and study field theory models on non-commutative (NC) space-times and analyse their quantization.
elements.sent-2411-012: In particular, construction of standard model in NC space-time is of immense importance and this was carried out in \cite{connes,landi .}
elements.sent-2411-013: These studies naturally led to the construction and investigation of gauge theories on NC space-time.
elements.sent-2411-014: NC standard model along with a consistent quantum gravity also opens up the possibility of grand unified models \cite{connes, landi .}
elements.sent-2411-015: As the existence of a fundamental length scale is not compatible with the special theory of relativity, principle of relativity needed to be modified.
elements.sent-2411-016: This lead to doubly special relativity (DSR) which admits a fundamental length scale and speed of light as universal constants \cite{DSR .}
elements.sent-2411-017: The underlying space-time of DSR is known to be $\kappa$-space- time \cite{Ruegg, Ruegg1,majid .}
elements.sent-2411-018: This $\kappa$-space-time also appears in the low energy limit of quantum gravity models \cite{freidel .}
elements.sent-2411-019: The symmetries of $\kappa$-space-time is realised as Hopf algebra \cite{Ruegg, Ruegg1 .}
elements.sent-2411-020: Various issues in constructing quantum field theories in $\kappa$-space-time are addressed and resolved in \cite{Ruegg, Ruegg1, majid,freidel,lukierski2,kosinski,kosinski1,kosinski2,amelino, MD jonke, jonke2, MD jonke1, wess,jonke3,jonke,jonke1 The present status of research on building gauge theories in various non-commutative space-times as well as attempts to include gravity into NC framework is discussed in \cite{hersent1 .}}
elements.sent-2411-021: Deformed gauge theories are constructed for arbitrary compact Lie groups and the Lagrangian for such gauge theories possessing a deformed Lorentz covariance are constructed in \cite{wess .}
elements.sent-2411-022: The symmetries of $\kappa$-space-time is realised using undeformed $\kappa$-Poincare algebra \cite{SM .}
elements.sent-2411-023: Here different possible realisations of space-time coordinates of $\kappa$-space-time in terms of the commutative variables are introduced.
elements.sent-2411-024: The studies of quantum field theories in $\kappa$-space time is carried out in \cite{EH, twisted,TRG,sivakumar, ED, geodesic .}
elements.sent-2411-025: Twisted flip operator which is relevant for describing particle statistics in $\kappa$-space-time is obtained in \cite{twisted .}
elements.sent-2411-026: In \cite{TRG , deformed statistics and oscillator algebra of quantum fields in $\kappa$-space-time are studied.}
elements.sent-2411-027: In \cite{sivakumar , the Dirac equation in $\kappa$-Minkowski space-time is constructed and it was shown that the charge conjugation invariance is lost while preserving parity and time reversal symmetries.}
elements.sent-2411-028: In these studies, $\kappa$-deformed space-time coordinates were written in terms of commutative coordinates and their derivatives.
elements.sent-2411-029: This provided a systematic way of writing down field theory models in $\kappa$-space-time in terms of commutative variables.
elements.sent-2411-030: This approach has the advantage of applying well established field theoretic tools of commutative space-time in analysing the effects of $\kappa$-deformation \cite{SM .}
elements.sent-2411-031: In \cite{dyson , Maxwell's equations were derived by assuming Newton's law and commutation relations between position coordinates and corresponding velocities.}
elements.sent-2411-032: Repeated use of Jacobi identity involving coordinates, velocities, and different combinations of coordinates and velocities then lead to two of the Maxwell's equations.
elements.sent-2411-033: The remaining equations were argued to be defining the sources of electric and magnetic fields, respectively, \cite{dyson .}
elements.sent-2411-034: This was followed by the derivation of Yang-Mills equation by considering a non-relativistic particle carrying isospin \cite{lee .}
elements.sent-2411-035: In\cite{tanimura , a covariant generalisation of the derivation of the Maxwell's equations and Yang-Mills equation were developed.}
elements.sent-2411-036: The construction of Yang-Mills equation in this approach crucially depends on the Wong's equation which describes the motion of a particle carrying isospin charge, in the presence of external non-abelian gauge field \cite{wongs .}
elements.sent-2411-037: Maxwell's equation in $\kappa$-space-time was derived by generalising Feynman's approach in \cite{EH .}
elements.sent-2411-038: Using the minimal coupling method, $\kappa$-deformed Maxwell's equation was derived in \cite{ED .}
elements.sent-2411-039: In this approach, corrections to Maxwell's equations, valid up to first order in the deformation parameter were derived.
elements.sent-2411-040: Here the deformed Maxwell's equations were written in terms of fields defined on the commutative space-time.
elements.sent-2411-041: Generalising this minimal coupling prescription to curved, $\kappa$-deformed space-time, geodesic equation in $\kappa$-deformed space-time was derived and analysed in \cite{geodesic .}
elements.sent-2411-042: Construction and analysis of non-abelian gauge theories in $\kappa$-space-time is an active area of research \cite{wess, kosinski,kosinski1,kosinski2,amelino,MD jonke1,MD jonke,jonke, jonke3,jonke2 .}
elements.sent-2411-043: In \cite{jonke3,jonke , a general formalism for constructing field theories on quantum space-time is developed and used to obtain field theory models on $\kappa$-space-time.}
elements.sent-2411-044: This was followed by the construction of U(1) gauge theory in $\kappa$-space-time using twist formalism \cite{MD jonke .}
elements.sent-2411-045: U(1) gauge theory on $\kappa$-Minkowski space is discussed in\cite{jonke2 .}
elements.sent-2411-046: The complications brought by the interconnection between translations and gauge symmetries in NC space-times in constructing Hodge-dual-field-strength tensor were addressed in \cite{MD jonke1 .}
elements.sent-2411-047: Using Seiberg-Witten map, the gauge theory on kappa-space-time was related to a theory defined on the commutative space-time having corrections depending on the deformation parameter.
elements.sent-2411-048: NC gauge theory has been constructed in $\kappa$-Minkowski space-time by applying Twist formalism in \cite{jonke1 .}
elements.sent-2411-049: In this work, we generalise the Feynman's approach in its covariant form to derive $\kappa$-deformed Yang-Mills equation.
elements.sent-2411-050: We also obtain the corresponding $\kappa$-deformed Yang-Mills Lagrangian.
elements.sent-2411-051: The $\kappa$-deformed Yang-Mills equation is derived by exploiting Jacobi identities involving various combinations of $\kappa$-deformed coordinates and corresponding velocities.
elements.sent-2411-052: In this approach, taking into account of the anti-symmetry property of the commutators between velocities, we introduce a second rank tensor, which is later shown to be the Yang-Mills field strength.
elements.sent-2411-053: The Jacobi identities then show that this Yang-Mills field strength satisfies homogeneous Yang-Mills equations.
elements.sent-2411-054: In evaluating the Jacobi identities, one needs the commutation relations between $\kappa$-deformed velocities with Lie algebra valued functions defined on $\kappa$-space-time.
elements.sent-2411-055: This naturally requires the commutation relation between different components of the $\kappa$-deformed velocities with generators of the Lie algebra and their time derivatives.
elements.sent-2411-056: The Wong's equation, describing the motion of isospin carrying particle in the background of non-abelian gauge field is used in the derivation of Yang-Mills equation at this stage.
elements.sent-2411-057: The Wong's equation comes with a condition on the time development of the generators of the Lie algebra associated with the non-abelian gauge field.
elements.sent-2411-058: This condition plays an important role in the derivation of Yang-Mills field equation using the Feynman's approach.
elements.sent-2411-059: Here we derive Yang-Mills equation in $\kappa$-space-time whose coordinate satisfy \be [\hat{x ^i,\hat{x ^j]=0, ~[\hat{x ^0, \hat{x ^i]=ia\hat{x ^i \ee where $a=\frac{1 {\kappa}$, by enlarging the scope of covariant generalisation of the Feynman's approach to NC space-time.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-060: This requires the derivation of $\kappa$-deformed Wong's equation.
elements.sent-2411-061: Using the $\kappa$-deformed Dirac Hamiltonian describing the interaction of isospin carrying fermion with non-abelian gauge field, we obtain the $\kappa$-deformed Wong's equation.
elements.sent-2411-062: With the aid of this, we find the commutation relation between $\kappa$-deformed velocity and a Lie algebra valued functions in the $\kappa$-space-time.
elements.sent-2411-063: This also serves to provide the explicit form of gauge covariant derivative.
elements.sent-2411-064: Now replacing $[\dot{\hat{x _{\mu , \dot{\hat{x _{\nu ]$ with a second rank, anti-symmetric tensor, in the Jacobi identity involving $\kappa$-deformed velocities, we obtain the equation satisfied by components of this second rank tensor.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-065: Employing Jacobi identities involving the deformed space-time coordinates and the generator of the Lie algebra, we find the second rank tensor introduced to be the Yang-Mills field strength in the $\kappa$-deformed space-time.
elements.sent-2411-066: Thus we derive the ``homogeneous'' Yang-Mills equation in the $\kappa$-deformed space-time.
elements.sent-2411-067: Using the $\kappa$-deformed field strength, we then write down the Lagrangian describing Yang-Mills field theory in the $\kappa$-deformed space-time.
elements.sent-2411-068: The remaining Yang-Mills equations follow as Euler-Lagrange equations from this $\kappa$-deformed Lagrangian.
elements.sent-2411-069: The force equation for a particle carrying isospin, interacting with Yang-Mills field is then derived.
elements.sent-2411-070: The derivation of $\kappa$-deformed Wong's equation also provides definition of gauge covariant derivative in $\kappa$-deformed space-time.
elements.sent-2411-071: We show that the commutators of this $\kappa$-deformed gauge covariant derivatives exactly match with the deformed Yang-Mills field strength we derived in the $\kappa$-space-time.
elements.sent-2411-072: We see that the gauge covariant derivatives and hence non-abelian ``electric'' and ``magnetic'' fields in the $\kappa$-space time are modified in the same general form but different in the numerical factors.
elements.sent-2411-073: This paper is organised as follows: In sec.2, a brief sketch of Feynman's approach of deriving Maxwell's equation and Yang-Mills equations are given.
elements.sent-2411-074: We find Wong's equation using the deformed Dirac Hamiltonian in $\kappa$-deformed space-time in sec.3.
elements.sent-2411-075: Using Wong's equation, we derive the commutation relation between Lie algebra valued functions and deformed velocities.
elements.sent-2411-076: We also obtain an expression for modified gauge covariant derivative in the $\kappa$-deformed space-time.
elements.sent-2411-077: Using this we derive the Yang-Mills field equation in $\kappa$ deformed space-time in sec.4.
elements.sent-2411-078: We also obtain the explicit expression of $su(n)$ field strength in this section.
elements.sent-2411-079: We obtain the corresponding $\kappa$-deformed Lagrangian describing Yang-Mills theory and discuss its symmetry in sec.5.
elements.sent-2411-080: We derive an expression for the deformed Lorentz force experienced by a isospin carrying particle in the presence of $su(n)$ gauge field which is discussed in sec.6.
elements.sent-2411-081: We give the concluding remarks in sec.7.

elements.sent-2411-150: We have constructed Yang-Mills theory in $\kappa$-deformed space-time, by generalising Feynman's approach.
elements.sent-2411-151: For this, we have first constructed the Wong's equation in $\kappa$-deformed space-time.
elements.sent-2411-152: We have obtained the force equation for a particle having isospin in presence of non-abelian gauge field.
elements.sent-2411-153: We have also constructed the Lagrangian for $\kappa$-deformed Yang-Mills theory and showed its invariance under SU(N) gauge transformations.
elements.sent-2411-154: Thus we see that the $\kappa$-deformed Yang-Mills theory obtained has same gauge invariance as its commutative counter part.
elements.sent-2411-155: In our study we have considered corrections valid only upto first order in the deformation parameter $a$.
elements.sent-2411-156: In our study, by considering the anti-symmetry property of commutators between velocities, we have introduced a second-rank, anti-symmetric tensor, which later found to be the Yang-Mills field strength tensor in $\kappa$-deformed space-time.
elements.sent-2411-157: Using the Jacobi identity repeatedly, we have showed that this second rank, anti-symmetric tensor obey homogeneous Yang-Mills field equation.
elements.sent-2411-158: In evaluating the Jacobi identity, we need commutation relation between Lie algebra valued functions and $\kappa$-deformed velocities, which in turn leads to the commutation relation between generators of Lie algebra and the velocities.
elements.sent-2411-159: This is obtained by using Wong's equation in $\kappa$-space-time, which we constructed using the $\kappa$-deformed Dirac Hamiltonian.
elements.sent-2411-160: By repeated use of Jacobi identity we have derived two of the homogeneous Yang-Mills field equation in $\kappa$-deformed space-time.
elements.sent-2411-161: We have obtained the explicit expression for $\hat{F _{0i}$ and $\hat{F _{ij}$ and found their $a$-dependent corrections.}}
